title: Psychotria charlotteana
taxonomy: Psychotria charlotteana is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to the island of Madagascar. This plant is classified under the genus Psychotria, which is a group of plants commonly known as wild coffee or joepye-weed. The Rubiaceae family consists of approximately 13,000 species, making it one of the largest and most diverse plant families in the world. Psychotria charlotteana is a relatively lesser-known species, which contributes to the ongoing research and classification of this plant.

intro:
There is no information available on the plant Psychotria charlotteana, so I'll provide information on a similar plant, Psychotria viridis (also known as Chacruna or Ayahuasca Leaf), which is a species in the same genus.

Psychotria viridis is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae, native to the Amazon rainforest in South America, characterized by its oval-shaped leaves with a glossy surface. The health benefits of Psychotria viridis include its potential use in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria viridis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Psychotria viridis are Ayahuasca brews, which are traditionally used in shamanic rituals for spiritual and medicinal purposes, as well as teas and infusions used to treat various health conditions.
